items.title: National Archives D-G: Over 10,000 items on Pak Lah gathered for planned memorial in Penang
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN, April 11 — The National Archives of Malaysia (ANM) has collected more than 10,000 materials related to former prime minister Abdullah Ahmad Badawi, which are currently being catalogued for placement at the Memorial Tun Abdullah Ahmad Badawi to be built at Vision Park in Bertam near here. Its director-general Zaidin Mohd Noor said the collection was carried out in collaboration with the Prime Minister’s Department (JPM) and comprises various official records, administrative documents, as well as materials related to the fifth prime minister’s involvement in politics and international relations. Previously, the Penang state government approved a parcel of land at Vision Park, Bertam, Kepala Batas for the construction of Memorial Tun Abdullah Ahmad Badawi in honour of the former prime minister’s services and legacy.

items.title: No 'happy ending' for Johor massage parlours in immigration raid
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: An immigration crackdown on suspected vice activities at two massage parlours has led to the detention of 22 people in raids across the state capital. Johor Immigration Department director Datuk Mohd Rusdi Mohd Darus said the arrests were made during a 3pm operation on April 9 under Ops Gegar, targeting two massage and reflexology centres. A total of 21 foreign nationals, aged between 18 and 40, were detained for suspected immigration offences.

items.title: US, Iran set for peace talks but doubts emerge over Lebanon, sanctions
items.summary: ISLAMABAD: The US and Iran were to hold negotiations in the Pakistani capital Islamabad on Saturday seeking to end their six-week-old war, although Teheran threw the talks into doubt by saying they could not begin without commitments on Lebanon and sanctions. He said talks would not start until those pledges were fulfilled. he said.

items.title: Federal Territories launches free aerobics programme to fight stress and obesity
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 11 — Prime Minister’s wife, Datuk Seri Dr Wan Azizah Wan Ismail, has urged city dwellers to join the 2026 Federal Territories Community Aerobics Programme (PSKWP2026) to help manage everyday stress. Minister in the Prime Minister’s Department (Federal Territories) Hannah Yeoh launched the programme, attended by Kuala Lumpur mayor Datuk Seri Fadlun Mak Ujud and Prime Minister’s political secretary Datuk Azman Abidin. The sessions run simultaneously at 25 public housing sites in Kuala Lumpur, expanding this weekend to 44: 33 in Kuala Lumpur, six in Putrajaya, five in Labuan, supported by partnerships with the Malaysia Fitness Association, residents’ councils, Kuala Lumpur City Hall, Putrajaya Corporation, Labuan Corporation and other agencies.

items.title: RM3.5m incentive launched to help palm oil traders meet sustainability standards, says minister
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 11 — The Ministry of Plantation and Commodities has introduced an incentive amounting to RM3.5 million in an effort to support oil palm fresh fruit bunches (FFB) traders in obtaining the Malaysian Sustainable Palm Oil (MSPO) certification. The Minister of Plantation and Commodities, Datuk Seri Noraini Ahmad, said this is a proactive step by the government to help oil palm FFB traders meet current compliance requirements, while also strengthening the resilience of the national palm oil supply chain. More than 3,000 eligible oil palm FFB traders nationwide are expected to benefit, subject to the stipulated terms and conditions, she said in a statement today.

items.title: Bera cops: Four more held over Triang kopitiam slashing attack, two to be charged
items.summary: KUANTAN, April 11 — Police have detained four more men suspected of involvement in an attack involving sharp weapons at a coffee shop in Triang, Bera on March 29. Bera police chief Supt Zulkiflee Nazir said all the suspects, aged between 27 and 37, surrendered themselves at the district police headquarters between April 6 and 8. He said one suspect was remanded from April 7 to 10, while two others were remanded from April 8 to April 13 to assist in investigations.

items.title: Pilot phase to iron out gaps in Malaysia’s new health insurance scheme, says deputy finance minister
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 11 — The Malaysian insurance and takaful industry is expected to address any structural gaps during the pilot phase of the Malaysia Health Insurance and Takaful Initiative (MHIT), which is scheduled for rollout in the second half of 2026. Deputy Finance Minister Liew Chin Tong is optimistic about the initiative and describes it as a significant step forward for the nation. The tentative terms are Tier 1 (good behaviour) and Tier 2 (premium and expensive) hospitals,” he said.

items.title: Three Malaysians arrested in Thailand over 97kg meth haul
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Three Malaysian men in their 20s have been arrested in northeastern Thailand with 97kg of crystal methamphetamine. Bukit Aman Narcotic Crime Investigation Department director Datuk Hussein Omar Khan said Thai police shared details of the arrests through the Narcotics Nucleus Secretariat at the border. (MORE TO COME) © New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

items.title: Police destroy four fish bombs found near Malaysia-Thailand border
items.summary: TANAH MERAH: Four improvised explosive devices (IEDs), believed to be fish bombs, were destroyed after being discovered near the Malaysia-Thailand border yesterday. He said a Strike Force team, assisted by personnel from GOF's Ninth Battalion, went to Pos Nurut to verify the discovery about 2.30pm. "Checks led to the discovery of four objects resembling fish bombs, believed to have been left at the base of an oil palm tree," he said.

items.title: Man charged after missed flight meltdown at KLIA T2 injures guard, damages property
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, April 11 — A 33-year-old foreign man who vandalised public facilities and injured a security officer in a recent viral incident at Terminal 2 (T2) of Kuala Lumpur International Airport (KLIA) has been arrested and charged in court. KLIA police chief ACP M Ravi said the suspect was arrested by KLIA district police personnel at about 10.30am at the scene while waiting for a transit flight to Hat Yai, Thailand. “During the incident, the suspect acted aggressively by striking a counter glass panel and a flight information board, throwing a drink bottle towards the counter, and injuring a security officer on duty,” he said in a statement today.

items.title: #NSTviral: Students' 30-second fight outside school goes viral
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: A 30-second video that captured a fight between two groups of secondary school students outside a school here has gone viral. The clip, which began circulating on social media yesterday, is believed to have been recorded outside a secondary school in Taman Selesa Jaya after school hours. They were seen wearing T-shirts bearing the school's credentials.

items.title: Promoter arrested over RM1,500 toy theft at Mid Valley
items.summary: Brickfields police chief Assistant Commissioner Hoo Chang Hook said the suspect was detained following a report lodged by the shop's sales manager on Friday. Hoo said closed-circuit television (CCTV) footage showed the suspect, a promoter for a supplier company, removing the items between 6.45pm and 7.50pm on April 7. The stolen items included various collectables, including Wolverine, Iron Man, Deadpool, and several Spider-Man variants, worth RM1,529.10.

items.title: Soldier in Pahang military camp assualt dies in hospital
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 11 — Prebet Muhammad Amirul Raziq Rosafindi, 24, who was previously reported to have been seriously injured in an alleged assault at a military camp in Pahang on March 31, has died while receiving treatment at a hospital in Temerloh, Berita Harian reported. His father, Rosafindi Tambi Chik, 49, said the victim breathed his last at 9.45am today, adding that the body would be buried at Pusara Damai Felda Sungai Kelamah in Gemas, Negeri Sembilan. Berita Harian reported that Muhammad Amirul Raziq had earlier been seriously injured in an alleged assault involving a corporal, believed to have stemmed from dissatisfaction over cleaning duties at a weapons store.

items.title: Govt to seek alternative materials to keep drug prices from soaring
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: The government will implement intervention measures and explore alternative raw materials to address the rising costs of medicines and medical devices caused by the global energy crisis. Economy Minister Akmal Nasrullah Mohd Nasir said the government is focusing on ensuring supply availability and finding alternatives to reduce dependence on existing sources. He said immediate steps are necessary to ensure the supply of medicines and health devices remains stable and to avoid disruptions to the national healthcare system.

items.title: [UPDATED] Anwar orders immediate assistance for Beluran school using tents as classrooms
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim has taken the plight of students at a school in Beluran to heart, directing the authorities to immediately assist them. Concerned with the fact that these students are forced to take lessons in tents, the prime minister ordered the Education Ministry (MoE) and the Implementation Coordination Unit of the Prime Minister's Department to solve the issue. Anwar included a link to a news article published by the Borneo Post, in which Likas assemblymen Tham Yun Fook had highlighted the plight of SK Balaban Jaya.

items.title: Rapid Rail fined RM100,000 over 2021 LRT crash
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Rapid Rail Sdn Bhd has been fined RM100,000 for the 2021 Light Rail Transit (LRT) collision that left 65 passengers injured. The Land Public Transport Agency (Apad) said the penalty follows investigation findings into maintenance shortcomings by the operator. Apad said Rapid Rail failed to ensure critical track components were in proper working condition.

items.title: [UPDATED] Bentong camp assault: Soldier dies 12 days after assault by corporal
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: A soldier who was allegedly assaulted by a junior non-commissioned officer at a camp in Bentong has died. Private Muhammad Amirul Raziq Rosafindi died at a hospital in Temerloh, Pahang, today. His father, Rosafindi Tambi Chik, confirmed the death when contacted.

items.title: 'Chow Kit is like a heaven for us'
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The back alleys of Chow Kit might be dreaded by outsiders, but they have become a "heaven" for Azza, a place where she can raise her three children. The young, single mother in her mid-30s with three children, has been raising her children there over the past 10 years. "The environment in Chow Kit is admittedly not suitable for building a family or raising children.

items.title: Diesel misappropriation syndicate using 4,500-litre tank lorry crippled
items.summary: SHAH ALAM: Authorities have crippled a syndicate that used a lorry fitted with a modified 4,500-litre tank to misappropriate subsidised diesel for the industrial market. The group used five Subsidised Diesel Control Scheme (SKDS) fleet cards belonging to various companies to refuel a five-tonne lorry at multiple petrol stations. During the raid, the syndicate had already pumped approximately 2,500 litres of diesel into a hidden tank equipped with a pumping system connected to the vehicle's original fuel tank.

items.title: Marine police seize diesel worth over RM1 million in Tawau
items.summary: TAWAU: The Marine Police Force in Tawau seized 30,800 litres of diesel worth RM1.16 million and arrested a local man in a raid at a warehouse on Jalan Sin San here yesterday. Sabah Marine Police Region 4 commander ACP Ahmad Amri Abd Rahman said the 5 pm raid was carried out based on PPM intelligence and a public tip-off, before trailing a lorry driven in a suspicious manner to the location. "Inspections found that illegal diesel transfer activities were taking place, and also uncovered three lorries, three skid tanks and 10 drum barrels believed to have been used for the misappropriation of controlled goods," he said in a statement here today.

items.title: Chinese national remanded over alleged sexual harassment near Kuchai Lama MRT station
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 11 — A Chinese national has been remanded until April 24 to assist investigations into an alleged sexual harassment case involving a woman at the MRT Kuchai Lama station pedestrian walkway here, Harian Metro reported. According to the Malay daily, Brickfields district police chief ACP Hoo Chang Hook said the 20-year-old suspect was arrested after police received a report on the incident at 9.22pm last Thursday. The suspect was arrested on the same day and has been remanded until April 24 to assist investigations.

items.title: Tabung Haji activates monitoring, scenario planning to protect Malaysian Haj pilgrims amid Middle East tensions
items.summary: SEPANG, April 11 — The safety of Malaysian Haj pilgrims and personnel remains a top priority for the government, particularly in facing any potential risks arising from geopolitical developments in West Asia, said Minister in the Prime Minister’s Department (Religious Affairs) Zulkifli Hasan. He said Lembaga Tabung Haji (TH) has established a special committee to monitor current developments and coordinate immediate action in the event of any emergency involving pilgrims. TH has comprehensive scenario planning,” he told the media after the send-off ceremony for the Haj personnel team here today.

items.title: Tow truck services expected to face three-day disruption as operators plan diesel subsidy strike
items.summary: In the same notice, Zurich Takaful said its internal towing operations will be extended to assist customers, with services arranged based on availability. Customers may also arrange for third-party towing services during the disruption, with reimbursement claims subject to terms and conditions. Perwatam president Datuk Khairi Anuar Ahmad said immediate and decisive action is needed to ensure towing services continue operating without disruption affecting road users.

items.title: Global equities remain primary driver of EPF returns
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The equity asset class, particularly overseas investments, remains the primary contributor to the Employees Provident Fund's investment returns. "The equity asset class remains the primary contributor to EPF's investment returns, especially overseas equities which benefited from the rapid growth of the technology and AI sectors, particularly in the US. "At the same time, the EPF equity portfolio is diversified across various geographies and sectors.

items.title: Outdoor activity SOPs must be preventive, not reactive, says Lee Lam Thye after Kedah drowning
items.summary: “Safety reviews must not be reactive. We must move from reactive safety to preventive safety. Safety must always come first.

items.title: Foreigner in viral KLIA2 ruckus video pleads guilty to hurting security officer
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: A foreigner seen in a viral video pushing a security officer in Kuala Lumpur International Airport Terminal 2 recently was arrested and charged with causing hurt and insulting behaviour. KLIA police chief Assistant Commissioner M. Ravi said the incident occurred on April 5, after the man had missed his connecting flight to Hat Yai. Ravi said the suspect pleaded guilty to a charge of voluntarily causing hurt under Section 323 of the Penal Code and a charge under Section 14 of the Minor Offences Act for insulting behaviour.

items.title: Marine police bust illegal diesel transfer in Tawau, seize over RM1m worth of fuel
items.summary: TAWAU, April 11 — The Marine Police Force (PPM) in Tawau seized 30,800 litres of diesel worth RM1.16 million and arrested a local man in a raid at a warehouse on Jalan Sin San here yesterday. Sabah PPM Region 4 commander ACP Ahmad Amri Abd Rahman said the 5 pm raid was carried out based on PPM intelligence and a public tip-off, before trailing a lorry driven in a suspicious manner to the location. “Inspections found that illegal diesel transfer activities were taking place, and also uncovered three lorries, three skid tanks and 10 drum barrels believed to have been used for the misappropriation of controlled goods,” he said in a statement here today.

items.title: Woman trapped, injured in four-vehicle crash in Sungai Buloh
items.summary: SUNGAI BULOH: A woman in her 30s was injured after becoming trapped in a four-vehicle pile-up near the Sungai Pelong police station here today. Selangor Fire and Rescue Department assistant director of operations Ahmad Mukhlis Mukhtar said a six-man team from the Sungai Buloh fire station was dispatched to the scene after receiving an emergency call. Ahmad Mukhlis said firemen arrived at 8.22am and immediately began extrication efforts.

items.title: USM students represent Malaysia at rocketry competition in US
items.summary: NIBONG TEBAL: A team of 10 students from Universiti Sains Malaysia (USM) is set to take Malaysian innovation to new heights as they prepare to compete in the International Rocket Engineering Competition 2026 (IREC 2026) in Midland, Texas, United States from June 15 to 20. Known as WAU Rocketry USM, the team will represent Malaysia alongside another team from Universiti Teknologi Malaysia (UTM) at one of the world's largest intercollegiate rocketry competitions, which bring together students from around the world to design, build and launch rockets. We launch a rocket and compete.

items.title: Kelantan migrant smuggling ring busted as repeat 'mastermind' rearrested
items.summary: KOTA BARU: The immigration authorities say they have dismantled a migrant smuggling operation in Kelantan, arresting 27 people, including a suspected repeat offender described as the group's "mastermind". A total of 22 foreign nationals were also detained. They comprised two Indian men, three Bangladeshi men, and 17 Myanmar nationals, including eight men and nine women.

items.title: A rocket, a dream and a shot at the stars: USM students head to global engineering competition in Texas
items.summary: NIBONG TEBAL, April 11 — A team of 10 students from Universiti Sains Malaysia (USM) is set to take Malaysian innovation to new heights as they prepare to compete in the International Rocket Engineering Competition 2026 (IREC 2026) in Midland, Texas, United States from June 15 to 20. Known as WAU Rocketry USM, the team will represent Malaysia alongside another team from Universiti Teknologi Malaysia (UTM) at one of the world’s largest intercollegiate rocketry competitions, which bring together students from around the world to design, build and launch rockets. We launch a rocket and compete.

items.title: Manager held after Johor restaurant raid over alleged cooking oil abuse
items.summary: Initial investigations revealed that the manager, in his 40s, had been cutting open subsidised cooking oil packets and transferring the oil into bottles for use in the restaurant's operations. Checks also found the restaurant had stored controlled goods, namely sugar, subsidised cooking oil and wheat flour, without a valid permit. The ministry seized 120kg of coarse sugar, 200kg of wheat flour and 51kg of subsidised 1kg packet cooking oil.

items.title: Palestinians still unsafe six months after Gaza truce, says UN rights chief
items.summary: UN High Commissioner for Human Rights Volker Turk said at least 738 Palestinians have been killed since the ceasefire began on Oct 10, 2025, including 32 deaths recorded since early April, according to figures from the Palestinian Ministry of Health. The UN human rights office has recorded 294 Palestinian journalists killed since Oct 7, 2023. Turk said 589 aid workers have been killed in Gaza since Oct 7, 2023, including 397 affiliated with the United Nations.

items.title: Tow truck operators to go on 3-day strike, demand diesel subsidies
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Tow truck services are expected to be disrupted for three days from tomorrow as operators will go on strike to urge the government to provide diesel subsidies. It said operating hours for internal tow trucks will be extended to assist customers, with scheduled services being carried out based on service availability. Perwatam president Datuk Khairi Anuar Ahmad, in a statement, said more realistic policy support is urgently needed to ensure the tow truck industry continues to operate.

items.title: Johor food outlet probed over alleged repackaging of subsidised cooking oil, two detained
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, April 11 — The Domestic Trade and Cost of Living Ministry (KPDN) exposed alleged misappropriation of subsidised packet cooking oil at a food premises in Taman Megah Ria, Masai, here during a 3pm operation on Thursday. Johor KPDN director Lilis Saslinda Pornomo said inspections found that the premises owner had cut open packets of cooking oil and transferred the contents into bottles. She said the premises was also found to be storing controlled items, namely sugar and wheat flour, as well as packet cooking oil, without a valid permit.

items.title: Unplanned development among the causes of Klang Valley traffic congestion
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: An increase in the number of private vehicles is not the sole cause of the worsening traffic congestion in the Klang Valley. Universiti Putra Malaysia Road Safety Research Centre head Professor Dr Law Teik Hua said this situation causes traffic flow to concentrate on specific routes, increasing pressure on the existing road networks. He had said, based on monitoring and Integrated Transport Information System (ITIS) data, the influx of vehicles into Kuala Lumpur between Feb 1 and 12 showed a significant increase, exceeding three million vehicles daily.

items.title: Sabah seeks Navy support to step up crackdown on fuel subsidy abuse
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, April 11 — The Ministry of Domestic Trade and Cost of Living (KPDN), Sabah branch, has requested that facilities of the Royal Malaysian Navy (RMN) be utilised to strengthen operations in combating the misappropriation of fuel subsidies, particularly in coastal and border areas of Sabah. State KPDN director Shahril Nizam Shahidin said the effort is seen as capable of helping the ministry expand its operational reach, especially in addressing smuggling activities and the misuse of controlled goods, such as petrol and diesel. “It serves as an important platform to strengthen bilateral cooperation, information sharing, and coordination of actions between KPDN and the RMN, towards enhancing the effectiveness of enforcement in tackling the misappropriation of government subsidies in an integrated manner.

items.title: Lebanon, Israel to hold first meeting at US State Dept Tuesday
items.summary: ISTANBUL: Lebanon and Israel agreed to hold their first meeting on Tuesday at the US State Department in Washington aimed at securing a ceasefire and launching direct talks, Anadolu Ajansi (AA) reported, citing the Lebanese presidency. As part of the process and based on instructions from Aoun, Lebanon's Ambassador to Washington Nada Hamadeh Moawad held a phone call with Israeli Ambassador to Washington Yechiel Leiter, in what was described as "the first" contact between the two sides. US Ambassador to Lebanon Michel Issa, who is currently in Washington, also participated in the call.

items.title: Malaysia-Turkiye defence ties should shift from deals to strategy, says academic
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 11 — An academic has urged Malaysia and Turkiye to strengthen defence cooperation by moving beyond procurement and establishing a strategic partnership to jointly develop the local defence and aerospace industry. With Malaysia being Turkiye’s seventh-largest defence partner and its oldest in South-east Asia, the strategic partnership should also include joint training programmes and research and development (R&D) to bolster Malaysia’s defence sector, said Kogila, alluding to the need for deepening defence cooperation given the tumultuous security situation due to the West Asia conflict. “Malaysia’s defence and foreign policy should integrate industrial strategy with Turkiye, emphasising a coherent localisation approach and considering offsets,” she said.

items.title: Guan Eng proposes loans, tax moratorium for MSMEs amid Middle East crisis
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: DAP adviser Lim Guan Eng has proposed five short-term financial measures to rescue micro, small, and medium enterprises (MSMEs) facing the global economic crisis. Lim said he submitted the proposals to Prime Minister and Pakatan Harapan chairman Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim at the coalition's Presidential Council meeting on Wednesday. Lim said that beyond volatile raw material prices, supply chain disruptions, and surging shipping and logistics costs, MSMEs were also grappling with liquidity issues.

items.title: Paka’s brackish water woes persist, as Terengganu authorities warn of months-long disruption
items.summary: DUNGUN, April 11 — Residents in Paka, near here, expressed disappointment with the water supply issue, which is alleged to taste brackish and rusty, and is affecting their daily routines since last month. “Moreover, at the same time, residents also frequently receive rusty water supply, especially when water pressure is low,” she said, when met by reporters, today. A restaurant operator in Tebing Tembah, Nurul Miezan Qamaliena Mazidi, 23, said she has been forced to buy mineral water to prepare drinks for customers, due to the brackish water issue.

items.title: Beautician molested near MRT station, foreigner arrested
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: A 20-year-old foreigner has been arrested for allegedly molesting a beautician near the Kuchai MRT station on Thursday night. Brickfields police chief Assistant Commissioner Hoo Chang Hook said the victim, 22, was approached by the suspect, an unemployed Chinese national, who hugged her from behind without her consent. Hoo said the suspect was arrested at 8.10pm the same day.

items.title: Leo to become first pope to visit Muslim Algeria
items.summary: VATICAN CITY: Leo XIV will become the first pope to visit Algeria on Monday, taking a message of dialogue with Islam on a trip that also represents a personal pilgrimage for the American pontiff. The visit also holds a strong personal dimension for Pope Leo, as modern-day Algeria was home to Saint Augustine (354-430), a great Christian theologian whose spiritual legacy permeates his pontificate. Vatican spokesman Matteo Bruni said the pope's aim was to "address the Islamic world, but also to confront a common challenge of coexistence."

items.title: Malaysia’s ancient past in focus as ‘Perak Man’ returns home to Lenggong and ‘Perak Woman’ eyed next
items.summary: LENGGONG, April 11 — The Perak Man skeleton, which was previously kept by the National Heritage Department in Kuala Lumpur, has been re-exhibited to the public at the Lenggong Valley Archaeological Gallery here since July. He said this after attending the reopening of the Lenggong Valley Archaeological Gallery and the launch of the World Heritage Site Commemorative Coin here yesterday. Perak Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Saarani Mohamad (5th left) views the ‘Perak Man’ skeleton at the reopening of the Lenggong Valley Archaeological Gallery in Lenggong, Perak, April 10, 2026.

items.title: Appeals Court reinstates Puncak Gali Harmoni’s suit against Emrail over RM145m Kelantan rail project, orders fresh hearing
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, April 11 — The Court of Appeal yesterday allowed the appeals by Puncak Gali Harmoni Sdn Bhd (PGH) to set aside the High Court’s decision to summarily dismissed its suit and ex parte injunction to prevent Emrail Sdn Bhd from terminating a subcontract award letter related to railway track restoration work between Gua Musang and Tumpat Stations in Kelantan. A three-judge panel led by Justice Datuk Mohamed Zaini Mazlan ordered the case to be reinstated and remitted to the High Court Shah Alam for the hearing of the originating summons as well as for the injunction application to be heard inter parte before another judge or judicial commissioner. In delivering the decision, Mohamed Zaini said the High Court had set aside the ex parte injunction even though PGH did not file an application to set it aside, adding that the absence of such an application, the High Court’s setting aside of the ex parte injunction was irregular.

items.title: Video of teen throwing brick from flat goes viral
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: A video of a teenager tossing a brick from the upper floor of a flat has gone viral. The 34-second closed-circuit television (CCTV) footage, dated yesterday, shows the youth walking towards a pile of bricks along a walkway and picking one up. The teenager then quickly ducks and waits for several seconds before continuing along the walkway.

items.title: Likas assemblyman urges urgent action as SK Balaban Jaya students study in tents and mosque corridors
items.summary: BELURAN, April 11 — Likas assemblyman Tham Yun Fook has called on both the state and federal education ministries to urgently address the plight of students at SK Balaban Jaya here, where students are reportedly attending classes in temporary and unsuitable learning spaces. Tham said classrooms at the school currently include wall-less tents and corridors of a mosque, as the school does not yet have a complete permanent building. He said he was informed that the Ministry of Education (MoE) has already allocated approximately RM2 million for the construction of a permanent school building.

items.title: Malaysia, Brunei boost collaboration on treatment, enforcement in anti-drug efforts
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN, April 11 — Malaysia and Brunei are to continue strengthening regional cooperation in the aspects of prevention, treatment, rehabilitation and enforcement to combat drug and substance abuse. AADK director-general Datuk Ruslin Jusoh said the meeting aimed to strengthen the network of cooperation between the two agencies, particularly in the sharing of best practices, experiences and innovative approaches in addressing the increasingly complex threat of drug and substance abuse. Following the meeting, Ruslin said AADK also expressed its willingness to continue sharing expertise with BKN Brunei Darussalam officers, especially involving aspects of human resource capacity development.

items.title: Eric Swalwell, candidate for California governor, denies sexual assault allegations
items.summary: Several leading Democratic politicians responded by urging Swalwell to end his campaign for governor. The former staffer later told CNN that Swalwell had raped her during the later encounter, which Swalwell strongly denied. Swalwell denied those claims as well.

items.title: ‘Something lived, not written’: Malaysia and Thailand strengthen bonds through culture, says Gobind
items.summary: PETALING JAYA, April 11 — Malaysia and Thailand continue to strengthen bilateral ties through cultural diplomacy with shared traditions and people connections, Digital Minister Gobind Singh Deo said. “What begins as cultural understanding often becomes economic strength and diplomatic trust,” he said. May this festival bring you peace, happiness and new beginnings,” he said.

items.title: IGP: Crime falls during Op Selamat for CNY, Raya 2026 but road accidents and fatalities rise
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 11 — Crime cases recorded a decrease to 747 during Op Selamat 25 and 26 this year, compared to 858 cases in 2025. For the best district awards, Miri was selected for Category A, Kuala Krai (Category B), and Jerantut (Category C). For the best district category, Johor Bahru Selatan was selected for Category A, Seberang Perai Selatan (Category B), and Bagan Datuk (Category C),” he said.

items.title: Sarawak’s Mulu and Niah national parks need major upgrade to unlock tourism potential, says councillor
items.summary: MIRI, April 11 — The federal government has been urged to reposition Sarawak’s iconic Mulu National Park and Niah National Park by upgrading their infrastructure to unlock their full tourism potential. Miri City Council’s Tourism Development Committee chairman Councillor Sean Huang hails both national parks as world-class tourist attractions, yet they have long been constrained by inadequate facilities, limited accessibility and insufficient promotion despite their global recognition. Huang stressed that the federal government must take decisive steps to reposition both parks as major tourism destinations by elevating infrastructure to national standards.

items.title: Trainee teacher, 25, killed in Tawau crash after car veers off road
items.summary: TAWAU, April 11 — A 25-year-old trainee teacher was killed in a road accident along Jalan Bandar Sri Indah here yesterday while on her way home from school, according to Sinar Harian. The victim, Amierah Aqeelah Arbin, who was also a final-year university student, was confirmed dead at the scene at about 12pm. Tawau Fire and Rescue Department chief Jemishin Ujin said the department received an emergency call at 12.26pm and deployed six personnel to the scene, about 17 kilometres away.

items.title: Govt to consider reviving price variation clause as construction costs surge, says deputy works minister
items.summary: SHAH ALAM, April 11 — A proposal to activate the Variation of Price (VOP) clause for construction materials will be submitted to the Finance Ministry and the Economy Ministry soon, following the global energy crisis, says Deputy Works Minister Datuk Seri Ahmad Maslan. He said the proposal stemmed from discussions with contractors’ associations last week, which reported an increase in construction material costs of between 20 and 30 per cent, although the matter still required further study before any decision could be made. We are (only) conveying the request from contractors’ associations to activate the VOP,” he said.

items.title: Former EC chairman Mohd Hashim Abdullah laid to rest in Putrajaya
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, April 11 — Former Election Commission (EC) chairman Tan Sri Mohd Hashim Abdullah was laid to rest at the Precinct 20 Muslim Cemetery here, at 10.20pm last night. Among those present to pay their last respects were Communications Ministry secretary-general Datuk Abdul Halim Hamzah, Putrajaya MP Datuk Radzi Jidin, former minister in the Prime Minister’s Department (Economy) Datuk Seri Mustapa Mohamed, former Prime Minister’s Department senior deputy secretary-general Tan Sri Othman Mahmood and former Treasury secretary-general Tan Sri Mohd Irwan Serigar Abdullah. Mohd Hashim, 71, a native of Kota Bharu, Kelantan, breathed his last breath at the National Heart Institute (IJN) at 2pm today due to health complications.

items.title: Americans give record-low marks to economy, in ominous sign for Republicans
items.summary: WASHINGTON: Americans are souring on the US economy to a degree never before seen, with the stiffest inflation in four years exacerbating the political risk for President Donald Trump, who some administration officials worry has lost focus on affordability problems for voters as he trains attention on the war against Iran. Few issues resonate with US voters more deeply than price increases, and the latest inflationary upswing is unsettling key insiders at the White House worried about their Republican Party's prospects in critical midterm elections less than seven months away. Republican lawmakers and senior White House aides have for months urged Trump to focus more on the economy, which is the top concern for voters.

items.title: Thai police arrest three Malaysians over 97kg meth haul on train to Bangkok
items.summary: BANGKOK, April 11 — Three Malaysian men were arrested in Thailand’s northeastern Khon Kaen province after about 97 kilogrammes (kg) of crystal methamphetamine were seized during a joint operation on a train bound for Bangkok, police said yesterday. In a statement yesterday, the Central Investigation Bureau (CIB) said the suspects were detained after officers found three unclaimed suitcases containing drugs on a train travelling from Nong Khai in northeast Thailand to Krung Thep Aphiwat Central Terminal in Bangkok. CIB said train staff later confirmed that three men had brought the suitcases on board at Nong Khai station, with CCTV footage identifying the suspects, who were later found seated in the same carriage.

items.title: Police warn public not to aid escapee from Sungai Buloh Prison
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 11 — The public is reminded not to conceal or abet the Pakistani accused person (OKT) who escaped from Sungai Buloh Prison on Friday. Sungai Buloh district police chief, Superintendent Mohd Hafiz Muhammad Nor said legal action could be taken under Section 216 of the Penal Code against any individual found to have assisted the OKT. Mohd Hafiz said the remand prisoner escaped while getting out of a police vehicle upon arrival at the Sungai Buloh Prison Parade Ground.

items.title: Govt to intensify cloud seeding in Melaka as first attempt yields limited impact, says DPM Zahid
items.summary: MELAKA, April 11 — Efforts to carry out cloud seeding in the southern part of Peninsular Malaysia, particularly in Melaka, will be intensified through several follow-up attempts after initial results were found to be less encouraging. Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Ahmad Zahid Hamidi said the cloud seeding attempt conducted in Melaka had minimal impact due to wind movement factors, which caused clouds to drift away from the targeted areas. Ahmad Zahid, who is also rural and regional development minister, said that the relevant parties would carry out second and third attempts to increase rainfall, which would help boost water supply in Melaka.

items.title: Artemis II astronauts return to Earth, capping historic Moon mission [WATCH]
items.summary: SAN DIEGO: The NASA spacecraft carrying four astronauts splashed down as planned Friday off the California coast, capping the US space agency's successful crewed test mission around the Moon, the first such flyby in more than 50 years. Mission commander Reid Wiseman reported that the crewmembers – himself along with Christina Koch, Victor Glover and Jeremy Hansen – were "stable" and "green." Following a brief but nerve-wracking communications blackout during their high-stakes re-entry, mission commander Wiseman's voice triggered relief that the astronauts were well on their way back home.
